(born 18 November 1985)[[1]](#cite_note-esbl-1) is an Estonian [long](/wiki/Long-distance_running "Long-distance running") and [middle](/wiki/Middle-distance_running "Middle-distance running")-distance runner, the current national record holder of the [1500 metres](/wiki/1500_metres "1500 metres") and [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian athletics competitor

Tiidrek Nurme

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Personal information | |
| Full name | Tiidrek Nurme |
| Born | (1985-11-18) 18 November 1985 (age 40)  [Kuressaare](/wiki/Kuressaare "Kuressaare"), [Estonian SSR](/wiki/Estonian_SSR "Estonian SSR"), Soviet Union |
| Height | 1.84 m (6 ft 0 in)[[1]](#cite_note-esbl-1) |
| Weight | 70 kg (154 lb)[[1]](#cite_note-esbl-1) |
| Sport | |

| Event(s) | [1500 m](/wiki/1500_meters "1500 meters"), [3000 m](/wiki/3000_meters "3000 meters"), [5000 m](/wiki/5000_meters "5000 meters"), [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |
| Achievements and titles | |
| Personal best | Marathon: 2:10:02 |

**Tiidrek Nurme** (born 18 November 1985)[[1]](#cite_note-esbl-1) is an Estonian [long](/wiki/Long-distance_running "Long-distance running") and [middle](/wiki/Middle-distance_running "Middle-distance running")-distance runner, the current national record holder of the [1500 metres](/wiki/1500_metres "1500 metres") and [3000 metres](/wiki/3000_metres "3000 metres").

Nurme is one of only five men in the history of the Olympic Games to have qualified for and competed in the 1,500 metres (2008) and the marathon (2016) in separate Olympics during their career. The other four are [Rod Dixon](/wiki/Rod_Dixon "Rod Dixon") of New Zealand (1972, 1984), [Luis Feiteira](/wiki/Luis_Feiteira "Luis Feiteira") of Portugal (1996, 2012), [Bayron Piedra](/wiki/Bayron_Piedra "Bayron Piedra") of Ecuador (2008, 2016) and [Jacques Boxberger](/wiki/Jacques_Boxberger "Jacques Boxberger") of France (1968, 1984).

At the Beijing 2008 Summer Olympics Nurme placed 27th among 50 runners (26th after a Moroccan got banned) in 1500m. In Beijing he set a national record with a time of 3:38.59, running at 1100m as a pacemaker.

At the Rio 2016 Summer Olympics, Nurme was part of the Olympic record-setting size marathon field when 155 men from 80 countries competed in the race where he placed 63rd overall.

At the [Tokyo 2020](/wiki/2020_Summer_Olympics "2020 Summer Olympics") Summer Olympics Nurme placed 27th among 106 runners (26th after a Moroccan got banned due to the [doping](/wiki/Doping_in_sport "Doping in sport") violation). Tokyo 2020 marathon was the deepest field in OG history, all 106 men had to be faster than the time of 2:11:30 to qualify.
